 54â†’public class FunctionSignatureRule :
 55â†’    StandardRule(
 56â†’        id = "function-signature",
 57â†’        visitorModifiers =
 58â†’            setOf(
 59â†’                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 60â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 61â†’            ),
 62â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 63â†’            setOf(
 64â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 65â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 66â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 67â†’                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 68â†’                FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY,
 69â†’            ),
 70â†’    ),
 71â†’    Rule.Experimental {
 72â†’    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 73â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 74â†’    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 75â†’    private var functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters =
 76â†’        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 77â†’    private var functionBodyExpressionWrapping = FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 78â†’
 79â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 80â†’        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 81â†’        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 82â†’        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]
 83â†’        indentConfig =
 84â†’            IndentConfig(
 85â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 86â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 87â†’            )
 88â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 89â†’    }
 90â†’
 91â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 92â†’        node: ASTNode,
 93â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 94â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 95â†’    ) {
 96â†’        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 97â†’            node
 98â†’                .functionSignatureNodes()
 99â†’                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
100â†’                .ifTrue {
101â†’                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
102â†’                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
103â†’                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
104â†’                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
105â†’                    // effort to attempt it.
106â†’                    return
107â†’                }
108â†’
109â†’            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
110â†’        }
111â†’    }

113â†’    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
114â†’        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
115â†’        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
116â†’        // or
117â†’        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
118â†’        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
119â†’        val startOfBodyBlock =
120â†’            this
121â†’                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
122â†’                ?.firstChildNode
123â†’        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
124â†’        return collectLeavesRecursively()
125â†’            .childrenBetween(
126â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
127â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
128â†’            )
129â†’    }
130â†’
131â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
132â†’        val funNode =
133â†’            if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
134â†’                this.treeParent
135â†’            } else {
136â†’                this
137â†’            }
138â†’        funNode
139â†’            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
140â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->
141â†’                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
142â†’                var currentNode: ASTNode
143â†’                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
144â†’                    currentNode = iterator.next()
145â†’                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
146â†’                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
147â†’                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
148â†’                    ) {
149â†’                        return currentNode
150â†’                    }
151â†’                }
152â†’                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
153â†’            }
154â†’        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
155â†’    }
156â†’
157â†’    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
158â†’        node: ASTNode,
159â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
160â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
161â†’    ) {
162â†’        require(node.elementType == FUN)
163â†’
164â†’        val forceMultilineSignature =
165â†’            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
166â†’                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
167â†’                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter())
168â†’        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
169â†’            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
170â†’            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
171â†’            // like below, which are not acceptable:
172â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
173â†’            //     ) = "some-value"
174â†’            //
175â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
176â†’            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
177â†’            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
178â†’            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
179â†’            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
180â†’            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
181â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
182â†’                if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
183â†’                    fixWhitespaceBeforeFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun = false)
184â†’                } else {
185â†’                    // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline signature needs to be
186â†’                    // recalculated
187â†’                    val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainingLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
188â†’                    fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
189â†’                }
190â†’            } else {
191â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
192â†’                if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
193â†’                    fixWhitespaceBeforeFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun = false)
194â†’                } else {
195â†’                    fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
196â†’                }
197â†’            }
198â†’        } else {
199â†’            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
200â†’            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
201â†’            // signature.
202â†’            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature =
203â†’                node
204â†’                    .functionSignatureNodes()
205â†’                    .none { it.textContains('\n') }
206â†’            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
207â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
208â†’            } else {
209â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
210â†’            }
211â†’        }
212â†’    }
213â†’
214â†’    private fun recalculateRemainingLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
215â†’        val closingParenthesis =
216â†’            node
217â†’                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
218â†’                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
219â†’        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature =
220â†’            node
221â†’                .functionSignatureNodes()
222â†’                .childrenBetween(
223â†’                    startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
224â†’                    endASTNodePredicate = { false },
225â†’                )
226â†’
227â†’        return node.indent(false).length +
228â†’            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
229â†’    }
230â†’
231â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
232â†’        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
233â†’            ?.children()
234â†’            .orEmpty()
235â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
236â†’            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
237â†’
238â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
239â†’        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
240â†’            ?.children()
241â†’            .orEmpty()
242â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
243â†’            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
244â†’
245â†’    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
246â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
247â†’            ?.children()
248â†’            .orEmpty()
249â†’            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
250â†’
251â†’    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
252â†’        node: ASTNode,
253â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
254â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
255â†’    ): Int {
256â†’        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
257â†’
258â†’        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
259â†’        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
260â†’        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
261â†’            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
262â†’            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true) +
263â†’            if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
264â†’                fixWhitespaceBeforeFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun = true)
265â†’            } else {
266â†’                0
267â†’            }
268â†’    }
269â†’
270â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = indent(false).length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
271â†’
272â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
273â†’        functionSignatureNodes()
274â†’            .joinTextToString()
275â†’            .length
276â†’
277â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
278â†’        node: ASTNode,
279â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
280â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
281â†’        multiline: Boolean,
282â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
283â†’    ): Int {
284â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
285â†’
286â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
287â†’        val firstParameterInList =
288â†’            valueParameterList
289â†’                .children()
290â†’                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
291â†’
292â†’        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
293â†’            if (firstParameterInList == null) {
294â†’                // handle empty parameter list
295â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
296â†’            } else {
297â†’                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
298â†’                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
299â†’                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
300â†’            }
301â†’
302â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
303â†’    }
304â†’
305â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
306â†’        node: ASTNode,
307â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
308â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
309â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
310â†’    ): Int {
311â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
312â†’
313â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
314â†’
315â†’        valueParameterList
316â†’            .children()
317â†’            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
318â†’            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
319â†’                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
320â†’                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
321â†’                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
322â†’            }
323â†’            .firstOrNull()
324â†’            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
325â†’                if (!dryRun) {
326â†’                    emit(
327â†’                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
328â†’                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
329â†’                        true,
330â†’                    )
331â†’                }
332â†’                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
333â†’                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
334â†’                } else {
335â†’                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
336â†’                }
337â†’            }
338â†’
339â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
340â†’    }
341â†’
342â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
343â†’        node: ASTNode,
344â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
345â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
346â†’        multiline: Boolean,
347â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
348â†’    ): Int {
349â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
350â†’
351â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
352â†’        val firstParameterInList =
353â†’            valueParameterList
354â†’                .children()
355â†’                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
356â†’
357â†’        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
358â†’        firstParameter
359â†’            ?.prevLeaf()
360â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
361â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
362â†’                if (multiline) {
363â†’                    val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
364â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
365â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
366â†’                    ) {
367â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
368â†’                            emit(
369â†’                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
370â†’                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
371â†’                                true,
372â†’                            )
373â†’                        }
374â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
375â†’                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
376â†’                        } else {
377â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
378â†’                        }
379â†’                    }
380â†’                } else {
381â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
382â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
383â†’                            emit(
384â†’                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
385â†’                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
386â†’                                true,
387â†’                            )
388â†’                        }
389â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
390â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
391â†’                        } else {
392â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
393â†’                        }
394â†’                    }
395â†’                }
396â†’            }
397â†’
398â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
399â†’    }
400â†’
401â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
402â†’        node: ASTNode,
403â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
404â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
405â†’        multiline: Boolean,
406â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
407â†’    ): Int {
408â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
409â†’
410â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
411â†’        val firstParameterInList =
412â†’            valueParameterList
413â†’                .children()
414â†’                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
415â†’
416â†’        valueParameterList
417â†’            .children()
418â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
419â†’            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
420â†’            .forEach { valueParameter ->
421â†’                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
422â†’                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
423â†’                    ?.prevLeaf()
424â†’                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
425â†’                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
426â†’                        if (multiline) {
427â†’                            val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
428â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
429â†’                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
430â†’                            ) {
431â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
432â†’                                    emit(
433â†’                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
434â†’                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
435â†’                                        true,
436â†’                                    )
437â†’                                }
438â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
439â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
440â†’                                } else {
441â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
442â†’                                }
443â†’                            }
444â†’                        } else {
445â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
446â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
447â†’                                    emit(
448â†’                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
449â†’                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
450â†’                                        true,
451â†’                                    )
452â†’                                }
453â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
454â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
455â†’                                } else {
456â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
457â†’                                }
458â†’                            }
459â†’                        }
460â†’                    }
461â†’            }
462â†’
463â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
464â†’    }

466â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
467â†’        node: ASTNode,
468â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
469â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
470â†’        multiline: Boolean,
471â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
472â†’    ): Int {
473â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
474â†’
475â†’        val newlineAndIndent = node.indent()
476â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
477â†’
478â†’        val closingParenthesis = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)
479â†’        closingParenthesis
480â†’            ?.prevSibling()
481â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
482â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
483â†’                if (multiline) {
484â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
485â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.text != newlineAndIndent
486â†’                    ) {
487â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
488â†’                            emit(
489â†’                                closingParenthesis!!.startOffset,
490â†’                                "Newline expected before closing parenthesis",
491â†’                                true,
492â†’                            )
493â†’                        }
494â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
495â†’                            closingParenthesis!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(newlineAndIndent)
496â†’                        } else {
497â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += newlineAndIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
498â†’                        }
499â†’                    }
500â†’                } else {
501â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
502â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
503â†’                    ) {
504â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
505â†’                            emit(
506â†’                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
507â†’                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
508â†’                                true,
509â†’                            )
510â†’                        }
511â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
512â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
513â†’                        } else {
514â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
515â†’                        }
516â†’                    }
517â†’                }
518â†’            }
519â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
520â†’    }

522â†’    private fun fixFunctionBodyExpression(
523â†’        node: ASTNode,
524â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
525â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
526â†’        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
527â†’    ) {
528â†’        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression =
529â†’            node
530â†’                .findChildByType(EQ)
531â†’                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
532â†’                ?: return
533â†’        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression)
534â†’        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
535â†’        val functionBodyExpressionNodes = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
536â†’
537â†’        val functionBodyExpressionLines =
538â†’            functionBodyExpressionNodes
539â†’                .joinTextToString()
540â†’                .split("\n")
541â†’        functionBodyExpressionLines
542â†’            .firstOrNull()
543â†’            ?.also { firstLineOfBodyExpression ->
544â†’                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
545â†’                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression
546â†’                        .nextCodeSibling()
547â†’                        .takeIf { it?.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION }
548â†’                        ?.let {
549â†’                            // Never merge an annotated expression body with function signature as this conflicts with the Annotation rule
550â†’                            return
551â†’                        }
552â†’                    val mergeWithFunctionSignature =
553â†’                        if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length < maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression) {
554â†’                            functionBodyExpressionWrapping == default ||
555â†’                                (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size == 1) ||
556â†’                                node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(
557â†’                                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression,
558â†’                                )
559â†’                        } else {
560â†’                            false
561â†’                        }
562â†’                    if (mergeWithFunctionSignature) {
563â†’                        emit(
564â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression!!.startOffset,
565â†’                            "First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature",
566â†’                            true,
567â†’                        )
568â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
569â†’                            (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
570â†’                        }
571â†’                    }
572â†’                } else if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
573â†’                    !whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.textContains('\n')
574â†’                ) {
575â†’                    if (node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression) &&
576â†’                        firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 <= maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression
577â†’                    ) {
578â†’                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
579â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.text != " "
580â†’                        ) {
581â†’                            emit(
582â†’                                functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
583â†’                                "Single whitespace expected before expression body",
584â†’                                true,
585â†’                            )
586â†’                            if (autoCorrect) {
587â†’                                functionBodyExpressionNodes
588â†’                                    .first()
589â†’                                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
590â†’                            }
591â†’                        }
592â†’                    } else if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 > maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression ||
593â†’                        (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size > 1) ||
594â†’                        functionBodyExpressionWrapping == always
595â†’                    ) {
596â†’                        emit(
597â†’                            functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
598â†’                            "Newline expected before expression body",
599â†’                            true,
600â†’                        )
601â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
602â†’                            functionBodyExpressionNodes
603â†’                                .first()
604â†’                                .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
605â†’                        }
606â†’                    }
607â†’                }
608â†’            }
609â†’    }

611â†’    private fun ASTNode.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression: ASTNode?) =
612â†’        functionSignatureNodes()
613â†’            .childrenBetween(
614â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { true },
615â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression },
616â†’            ).joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
617â†’            .split("\n")
618â†’            .lastOrNull()
619â†’            ?.matches(INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS)
620â†’            ?: false
621â†’
622â†’    private fun fixWhitespaceBeforeFunctionBodyBlock(
623â†’        node: ASTNode,
624â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
625â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
626â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
627â†’    ): Int {
628â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
629â†’
630â†’        node
631â†’            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
632â†’            ?.takeIf { it.findChildByType(LBRACE) != null }
633â†’            ?.let { block ->
634â†’                block
635â†’                    .prevLeaf()
636â†’                    .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
637â†’                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeBlock ->
638â†’                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeBlock == null || whiteSpaceBeforeBlock.text != " ") {
639â†’                            if (!dryRun) {
640â†’                                emit(block.startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
641â†’                            }
642â†’                            if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
643â†’                                block.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
644â†’                            } else {
645â†’                                whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeBlock?.textLength ?: 0)
646â†’                            }
647â†’                        }
648â†’                    }
649â†’            }
650â†’
651â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
652â†’    }

654â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionBody(splitNode: ASTNode?): List<ASTNode> =
655â†’        this
656â†’            .collectLeavesRecursively()
657â†’            .childrenBetween(
658â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == splitNode },
659â†’                endASTNodePredicate = {
660â†’                    // collect all remaining nodes
661â†’                    false
662â†’                },
663â†’            )

665â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull() =
666â†’        this
667â†’            .firstOrNull()
668â†’            ?.takeIf { first -> first.isWhiteSpace() }

674â†’    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =

682â†’
683â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
684â†’        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
685â†’        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
686â†’    ): List<ASTNode> {
687â†’        val iterator = iterator()
688â†’        var currentNode: ASTNode
689â†’        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
690â†’
691â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
692â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
693â†’            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
694â†’                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
695â†’                break
696â†’            }
697â†’        }
698â†’
699â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
700â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
701â†’            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
702â†’            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
703â†’                break
704â†’            }
705â†’        }
706â†’
707â†’        return childrenBetween
708â†’    }
709â†’
710â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(block: (ASTNode) -> String = { it.text }): String =
711â†’        collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { block(it) }
712â†’
713â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters
714â†’
715â†’    private fun ASTNode.countParameters(): Int {
716â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
717â†’        return valueParameterList
718â†’            .children()
719â†’            .count { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
720â†’    }
721â†’
722â†’    private fun ASTNode.getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody(): ASTNode? =
723â†’        this
724â†’            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
725â†’            ?.firstChildNode
726â†’            ?.prevCodeLeaf()
727â†’
728â†’    public companion object {
729â†’        private const val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET = Int.MAX_VALUE
730â†’        public val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
731â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
732â†’                type =
733â†’                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
734â†’                        "ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than",
735â†’                        "Force wrapping the parameters of the function signature in case it contains at least the specified " +
736â†’                            "number of parameters even in case the entire function signature would fit on a single line. " +
737â†’                            "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
738â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
739â†’                        setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
740â†’                    ),
741â†’                defaultValue = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET,
742â†’                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 2,
743â†’                propertyMapper = { property, _ ->
744â†’                    if (property?.isUnset == true) {
745â†’                        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET
746â†’                    } else {
747â†’                        property?.getValueAs<Int>()
748â†’                    }
749â†’                },
750â†’                propertyWriter = { property ->
751â†’                    if (property == FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET) {
752â†’                        "unset"
753â†’                    } else {
754â†’                        property.toString()
755â†’                    }
756â†’                },
757â†’            )
758â†’
759â†’        public val FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping> =
760â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
761â†’                type =
762â†’                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
763â†’                        "ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping",
764â†’                        "Determines how to wrap the body of function in case it is an expression. Use 'default' " +
765â†’                            "to wrap the body expression only when the first line of the expression does not fit on the same " +
766â†’                            "line as the function signature. Use 'multiline' to force wrapping of body expressions that " +
767â†’                            "consists of multiple line. Use 'always' to force wrapping of body expression always.",
768â†’                        EnumValueParser(FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping::class.java),
769â†’                        FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
770â†’                    ),
771â†’                defaultValue = default,
772â†’                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = multiline,
773â†’            )
774â†’
775â†’        private val INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS = Regex("\\s*\\) =")
776â†’    }
777â†’
778â†’    /**
779â†’     * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
780â†’     */
781â†’    @Suppress("EnumEntryName")
782â†’    public enum class FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping {
783â†’        /**
784â†’         * Keep the first line of the body expression on the same line as the function signature if max line length is
785â†’         * not exceeded.
786â†’         */
787â†’        default,
788â†’
789â†’        /**
790â†’         * Force the body expression to start on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line
791â†’         * body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
792â†’         */
793â†’        multiline,
794â†’
795â†’        /**
796â†’         * Always force the body expression to start on a separate line.
797â†’         */
798â†’        always,
799â†’    }
800â†’}
801â†’
802â†’public val FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionSignatureRule().ruleId
803â†’

1â†’In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2â†’
 3â†’```ini
 4â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 5â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6â†’```
 7â†’
 8â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9â†’
10â†’!!! note
11â†’    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12â†’
13â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14â†’
15â†’* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16â†’
17â†’* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18â†’

If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`."}

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
  2â†’
  3â†’!!! danger
  4â†’
  5â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
  6â†’
  7â†’## Code style
  8â†’
  9â†’By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
 10â†’
 11â†’```ini
 12â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 13â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 14â†’```
 15â†’
 16â†’!!! note
 17â†’    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
 18â†’
 19â†’## Disabled rules
 20â†’
 21â†’!!! note
 22â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
 23â†’
 24â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
 25â†’
 26â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 27â†’```editorconfig
 28â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 29â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
 30â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 31â†’```
 32â†’
 33â†’Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
 34â†’```editorconfig
 35â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 36â†’```
 37â†’
 38â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 39â†’```editorconfig
 40â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
 41â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 42â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 43â†’```
 44â†’
 45â†’!!! note
 46â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 47â†’
 48â†’
 49â†’## Final newline
 50â†’
 51â†’By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
 52â†’
 53â†’```ini
 54â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 55â†’insert_final_newline = true
 56â†’```
 57â†’
 58â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
 59â†’
 60â†’## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters
 61â†’
 62â†’Setting `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` forces a multiline function signature in case the function contains the specified minimum number of parameters even in case the function signature would fit on a single line. Use value `unset` (default) to disable this setting.
 63â†’
 64â†’!!! note
 65â†’    By default, the `ktlint_official` code style wraps parameters of functions having at least 2 parameters. For other code styles, this setting is disabled by default. 
 66â†’
 67â†’```ini
 68â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 69â†’ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than=unset
 70â†’```
 71â†’
 72â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
 73â†’
 74â†’## Wrapping the expression body of a function
 75â†’
 76â†’Setting `ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping` determines if and when the expression body of a function is wrapped to a new line. This setting can be set to value `default`, `multiline` or `always`. 
 77â†’
 78â†’When set to `default`, the first line of a body expression is appended to the function signature as long as the max line length is not exceeded.
 79â†’
 80â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=default (or when not set)"
 81â†’// Given that the function signature has to be written as a single line function signature
 82â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 83â†’    .uppercase()
 84â†’
 85â†’// Given that the function signature has to be written as a multiline function signature
 86â†’fun someFunction(
 87â†’    a: Any,
 88â†’    b: Any
 89â†’): String = "some-result"
 90â†’    .uppercase()
 91â†’```
 92â†’
 93â†’When set to `multiline`, the body expression starts on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
 94â†’
 95â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=multiline"
 96â†’// Given a single line body expression and
 97â†’// a the function signature that has to be written as a single line function signature and
 98â†’// it does not exceed the max line length
 99â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result".uppercase()
100â†’
101â†’// Given a single line body expression and
102â†’// a the function signature that has to be written as a multiline function signature and
103â†’// it does not exceed the max line length
104â†’fun someFunction(
105â†’    a: Any,
106â†’    b: Any
107â†’): String = "some-result".uppercase()
108â†’
109â†’// Given a single line body expression then always wrap it to a separate line
110â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
111â†’    "some-result"
112â†’         .uppercase()
113â†’fun someFunction(
114â†’    a: Any,
115â†’    b: Any
116â†’): String =
117â†’    "some-result"
118â†’       .uppercase()
119â†’```
120â†’
121â†’When set to `always` the body expression is always wrapped to a separate line.
122â†’
123â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=always"
124â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
125â†’    "some-result".uppercase()
126â†’fun functionWithAVeryLongName(
127â†’    a: Any,
128â†’    b: Any
129â†’): String =
130â†’    "some-result"
131â†’        .uppercase()
132â†’```
133â†’
134â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
135â†’
136â†’## Ignore identifiers enclosed in backticks
137â†’
138â†’By default, the identifiers enclosed in backticks are not ignored.
139â†’
140â†’According to [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) it is acceptable to write method names in natural language. When using natural language, the description tends to be longer. This property allows lines containing an identifier between backticks to be longer than the maximum line length. (Since 0.41.0)
141â†’
142â†’```kotlin
143â†’@Test
144â†’fun `Given a test with a very loooooooooooooooooooooong test description`() {
145â†’    
146â†’}
147â†’```
148â†’
149â†’```ini
150â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
151â†’ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
152â†’```
153â†’
154â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
155â†’
156â†’## Import layouts
157â†’
158â†’By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
159â†’
160â†’The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
161â†’
162â†’*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
163â†’* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
164â†’* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
165â†’
166â†’Examples:
167â†’```kotlin
168â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
169â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
170â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
171â†’```
172â†’
173â†’Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
174â†’
175â†’```ini
176â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
177â†’ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
178â†’```
179â†’
180â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
181â†’
182â†’## Indent size & style
183â†’
184â†’By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level. Code style `android_studio` uses a tab per indent level.
185â†’
186â†’```ini
187â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
188â†’indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
189â†’indent_style = space # or "tab"
190â†’```
191â†’
192â†’Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
193â†’
194â†’## Max line length
195â†’
196â†’By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
197â†’
198â†’```ini
199â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
200â†’max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
201â†’```
202â†’
203â†’This setting is used by multiple rules of which rule `max-line-length` is the most important.
204â†’
205â†’## Trailing comma on call site
206â†’
207â†’KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at call site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
208â†’
209â†’!!! note
210â†’    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
211â†’
212â†’    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
213â†’    
214â†’     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner â€“ as all the focus is on the changed value.
215â†’     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements â€“ there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
216â†’     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
217â†’
218â†’Example:
219â†’```ini
220â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
221â†’ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
222â†’```
223â†’
224â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-call-site` is enabled.
225â†’
226â†’## Trailing comma on declaration site
227â†’
228â†’KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
229â†’
230â†’!!! note
231â†’    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
232â†’
233â†’    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) encourages the usage of trailing commas on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
234â†’    
235â†’     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner â€“ as all the focus is on the changed value.
236â†’     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements â€“ there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
237â†’     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
238â†’
239â†’Example:
240â†’```ini
241â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
242â†’ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false # Only used for declaration site 
243â†’```
244â†’
245â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` is enabled.
246â†’
247â†’## Overriding Editorconfig properties for specific directories
248â†’
249â†’You can [override](https://editorconfig.org/#file-format-details) properties for specific directories inside your project:
250â†’```ini
251â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
252â†’ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
253â†’
254â†’[api/*.{kt,kts}]
255â†’ktlint_standard_indent = disabled
256â†’```
257â†’
258â†’Note that the `import-ordering` rule is disabled for *all* packages including the `api` sub package. Next to this the `indent` rule is disabled for the `api` package and its sub packages.
259â†’

1â†’In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2â†’
 3â†’```ini
 4â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 5â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6â†’```
 7â†’
 8â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9â†’
10â†’!!! note
11â†’    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12â†’
13â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14â†’
15â†’* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16â†’
17â†’* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18â†’

{"old_string":"In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).

```ini
[*.{kt,kts}]

This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.

180â†’
181â†’By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level. Code style `android_studio` uses a tab per indent level.
182â†’
183â†’```ini
184â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
185â†’indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
186â†’indent_style = space # or "tab"
187â†’```
188â†’
189â†’Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
190â†’
191â†’## Max line length
192â†’
193â†’By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
194â†’
195â†’```ini
196â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
197â†’max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
198â†’```

1â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
  2â†’
  3â†’!!! danger
  4â†’
  5â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
  6â†’
  7â†’## Code style
  8â†’
  9â†’By default, the `ktlint_official` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `intellij_idea` or `android_studio`.
 10â†’
 11â†’```ini
 12â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 13â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 14â†’```
 15â†’
 16â†’## Disabled rules
 17â†’
 18â†’!!! note
 19â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
 20â†’
 21â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
 22â†’
 23â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 24â†’```editorconfig
 25â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 26â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
 27â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 28â†’```
 29â†’
 30â†’Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
 31â†’```editorconfig
 32â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 33â†’```
 34â†’
 35â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 36â†’```editorconfig
 37â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
 38â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 39â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 40â†’```
 41â†’
 42â†’!!! note
 43â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 44â†’
 45â†’
 46â†’## Final newline
 47â†’
 48â†’By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
 49â†’
 50â†’```ini
 51â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 52â†’insert_final_newline = true
 53â†’```
 54â†’
 55â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
 56â†’
 57â†’## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters
 58â†’
 59â†’Setting `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` forces a multiline function signature in case the function contains the specified minimum number of parameters even in case the function signature would fit on a single line. Use value `unset` (default) to disable this setting.
 60â†’
 61â†’!!! note
 62â†’    By default, the `ktlint_official` code style wraps parameters of functions having at least 2 parameters. For other code styles, this setting is disabled by default. 
 63â†’
 64â†’```ini
 65â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 66â†’ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than=unset
 67â†’```
 68â†’
 69â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
 70â†’
 71â†’## Wrapping the expression body of a function
 72â†’
 73â†’Setting `ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping` determines if and when the expression body of a function is wrapped to a new line. This setting can be set to value `default`, `multiline` or `always`. 
 74â†’
 75â†’When set to `default`, the first line of a body expression is appended to the function signature as long as the max line length is not exceeded.
 76â†’
 77â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=default (or when not set)"
 78â†’// Given that the function signature has to be written as a single line function signature
 79â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 80â†’    .uppercase()
 81â†’
 82â†’// Given that the function signature has to be written as a multiline function signature
 83â†’fun someFunction(
 84â†’    a: Any,
 85â†’    b: Any
 86â†’): String = "some-result"
 87â†’    .uppercase()
 88â†’```
 89â†’
 90â†’When set to `multiline`, the body expression starts on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
 91â†’
 92â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=multiline"
 93â†’// Given a single line body expression and
 94â†’// a the function signature that has to be written as a single line function signature and
 95â†’// it does not exceed the max line length
 96â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result".uppercase()
 97â†’
 98â†’// Given a single line body expression and
 99â†’// a the function signature that has to be written as a multiline function signature and
100â†’// it does not exceed the max line length
101â†’fun someFunction(
102â†’    a: Any,
103â†’    b: Any
104â†’): String = "some-result".uppercase()
105â†’
106â†’// Given a single line body expression then always wrap it to a separate line
107â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
108â†’    "some-result"
109â†’         .uppercase()
110â†’fun someFunction(
111â†’    a: Any,
112â†’    b: Any
113â†’): String =
114â†’    "some-result"
115â†’       .uppercase()
116â†’```
117â†’
118â†’When set to `always` the body expression is always wrapped to a separate line.
119â†’
120â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=always"
121â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
122â†’    "some-result".uppercase()
123â†’fun functionWithAVeryLongName(
124â†’    a: Any,
125â†’    b: Any
126â†’): String =
127â†’    "some-result"
128â†’        .uppercase()
129â†’```
130â†’
131â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
132â†’
133â†’## Ignore identifiers enclosed in backticks
134â†’
135â†’By default, the identifiers enclosed in backticks are not ignored.
136â†’
137â†’According to [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) it is acceptable to write method names in natural language. When using natural language, the description tends to be longer. This property allows lines containing an identifier between backticks to be longer than the maximum line length. (Since 0.41.0)
138â†’
139â†’```kotlin
140â†’@Test
141â†’fun `Given a test with a very loooooooooooooooooooooong test description`() {
142â†’    
143â†’}
144â†’```
145â†’
146â†’```ini
147â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
148â†’ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
149â†’```
150â†’
151â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
152â†’
153â†’## Import layouts
154â†’
155â†’By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
156â†’
157â†’The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
158â†’
159â†’*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
160â†’* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
161â†’* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
162â†’
163â†’Examples:
164â†’```kotlin
165â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
166â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
167â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
168â†’```
169â†’
170â†’Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
171â†’
172â†’```ini
173â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
174â†’ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
175â†’```
176â†’
177â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
178â†’
179â†’## Indent size & style
180â†’
181â†’By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level. Code style `android_studio` uses a tab per indent level.
182â†’
183â†’```ini
184â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
185â†’indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
186â†’indent_style = space # or "tab"
187â†’```
188â†’
189â†’Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
190â†’
191â†’## Max line length
192â†’
193â†’By default, the maximum line length is set to 140 (as defined in `ktlint_official` code style). The `android_studio` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide). For the `intellij_idea` code style the maximum line length is not set.
194â†’
195â†’```ini
196â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
197â†’max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
198â†’```
199â†’
200â†’This setting is used by multiple rules of which rule `max-line-length` is the most important.
201â†’
202â†’## Trailing comma on call site
203â†’
204â†’KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at call site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
205â†’
206â†’!!! note
207â†’    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
208â†’
209â†’    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
210â†’    
211â†’     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner â€“ as all the focus is on the changed value.
212â†’     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements â€“ there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
213â†’     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
214â†’
215â†’Example:
216â†’```ini
217â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
218â†’ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
219â†’```
220â†’
221â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-call-site` is enabled.
222â†’
223â†’## Trailing comma on declaration site
224â†’
225â†’KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
226â†’
227â†’!!! note
228â†’    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
229â†’
230â†’    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) encourages the usage of trailing commas on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
231â†’    
232â†’     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner â€“ as all the focus is on the changed value.
233â†’     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements â€“ there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
234â†’     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
235â†’
236â†’Example:
237â†’```ini
238â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
239â†’ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false # Only used for declaration site 
240â†’```
241â†’
242â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` is enabled.
243â†’
244â†’## Overriding Editorconfig properties for specific directories
245â†’
246â†’You can [override](https://editorconfig.org/#file-format-details) properties for specific directories inside your project:
247â†’```ini
248â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
249â†’ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
250â†’
251â†’[api/*.{kt,kts}]
252â†’ktlint_standard_indent = disabled
253â†’```
254â†’
255â†’Note that the `import-ordering` rule is disabled for *all* packages including the `api` sub package. Next to this the `indent` rule is disabled for the `api` package and its sub packages.
256â†’

 28â†’/**
 29â†’ * Linting trailing comma for call site.
 30â†’ *
 31â†’ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 32â†’ */
 33â†’public class TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule :
 34â†’    StandardRule(
 35â†’        id = "trailing-comma-on-call-site",
 36â†’        visitorModifiers =
 37â†’            setOf(
 38â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 39â†’                    ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 40â†’                    mode = ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED,
 41â†’                ),
 42â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 43â†’            ),
 44â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY),
 45â†’    ) {
 46â†’    private var allowTrailingCommaOnCallSite = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 47â†’
 48â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49â†’        allowTrailingCommaOnCallSite = editorConfig[TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY]
 50â†’    }
 51â†’
 52â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 53â†’        node: ASTNode,
 54â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 55â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 56â†’    ) {
 57â†’        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 58â†’        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 59â†’        when (node.elementType) {
 60â†’            COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION -> visitCollectionLiteralExpression(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 61â†’            INDICES -> visitIndices(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 62â†’            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 63â†’            VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 64â†’            else -> Unit
 65â†’        }
 66â†’    }
 67â†’

 76â†’                .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RBRACKET }
 77â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(

 83â†’    }

102â†’    }
103â†’
