115â†’                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
116â†’                        emit = emit,
117â†’                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
118â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
119â†’                    )
120â†’                }
121â†’        }
122â†’    }
123â†’
124â†’    private fun visitTypeList(
125â†’        node: ASTNode,
126â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128â†’    ) {
129â†’        val inspectNode =
130â†’            node
131â†’                .children()
132â†’                .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
133â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
134â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
135â†’            emit = emit,
136â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
137â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
138â†’        )
139â†’    }
140â†’
141â†’    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
142â†’        inspectNode: ASTNode,
143â†’        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
144â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
145â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
146â†’    ) {
147â†’        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
148â†’        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
149â†’        val trailingCommaState =
150â†’            when {
151â†’                this.isMultiline() -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
152â†’                else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
153â†’            }
154â†’        when (trailingCommaState) {
155â†’            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
156â†’                if (!isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
157â†’                    emit(
158â†’                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
159â†’                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
160â†’                        true,
161â†’                    )
162â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
163â†’                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
164â†’                    }
165â†’                }
166â†’            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
167â†’                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
168â†’                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
169â†’                    emit(
170â†’                        prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
171â†’                        "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
172â†’                        true,
173â†’                    )
174â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
175â†’                        inspectNode
176â†’                            .prevCodeSibling()
177â†’                            ?.nextSibling()
178â†’                            ?.let { before ->
179â†’                                before.treeParent.addChild(LeafPsiElement(COMMA, ","), before)
180â†’                            }
181â†’                    }
182â†’                }
183â†’            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
184â†’                emit(
185â†’                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
186â†’                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
187â†’                    true,
188â†’                )
189â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
190â†’                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
191â†’                }
192â†’            }
193â†’            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
194â†’        }
195â†’    }
196â†’
197â†’    private fun ASTNode.isMultiline(): Boolean =
198â†’        if (elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
199â†’            hasAtLeastOneArgument() && hasValueArgumentFollowedByWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
200â†’        } else {
201â†’            textContains('\n')
202â†’        }
203â†’
204â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasValueArgumentFollowedByWhiteSpaceWithNewline(): Boolean =
205â†’        findValueArgumentFollowedByWhiteSpaceWithNewline() != null
206â†’
207â†’    private fun ASTNode.findValueArgumentFollowedByWhiteSpaceWithNewline() =
208â†’        this
209â†’            .findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT)
210â†’            ?.nextSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
211â†’
212â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasAtLeastOneArgument() = children().any { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT }
213â†’
214â†’    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
215â†’        val codeLeaf =
216â†’            if (isCodeLeaf()) {
217â†’                this
218â†’            } else {
219â†’                prevCodeLeaf()
220â†’            }
221â†’        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
222â†’    }
223â†’
224â†’    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
225â†’        /**
226â†’         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
227â†’         */
228â†’        EXISTS,
229â†’
230â†’        /**
231â†’         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
232â†’         */
233â†’        MISSING,
234â†’
235â†’        /**
236â†’         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
237â†’         */
238â†’        NOT_EXISTS,
239â†’
240â†’        /**
241â†’         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
242â†’         */
243â†’        REDUNDANT,
244â†’    }
245â†’
246â†’    public companion object {
247â†’        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
248â†’
249â†’        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
250â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
251â†’                type =
252â†’                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
253â†’                        "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site",
254â†’                        "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the calling side," +
255â†’                            "e.g. argument-list, when-entries, lambda-arguments, indices, etc." +
256â†’                            "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
257â†’                            "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
258â†’                            "trailing comma when set.",
259â†’                        PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
260â†’                        BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
261â†’                    ),
262â†’                defaultValue = true,
263â†’                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = false,
264â†’            )
265â†’
266â†’        private val TYPES_ON_CALL_SITE =
267â†’            TokenSet.create(
268â†’                COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION,
269â†’                INDICES,
270â†’                TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST,
271â†’                VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST,
272â†’            )
273â†’    }
274â†’}
275â†’
276â†’public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID: RuleId = TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule().ruleId
277â†’

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ARROW
  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS
  6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COMMA
  7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUNCTION_TYPE
 10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RBRACE
 11â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.SEMICOLON
 12â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST
 13â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
 14â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHEN_ENTRY
 15â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
 16â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED
 17â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 18â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 19â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 20â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 21â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 22â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isCodeLeaf
 23â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 24â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.noNewLineInClosedRange
 25â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 26â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 27â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 28â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.util.cast
 29â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 30â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 31â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser
 32â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 33â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 34â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 35â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 36â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 37â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 38â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtClass
 39â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCollectionLiteralExpression
 40â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtDestructuringDeclaration
 41â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtEnumEntry
 42â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFunctionLiteral
 43â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtParameterList
 44â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtValueArgumentList
 45â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhenEntry
 46â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhenExpression
 47â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.anyDescendantOfType
 48â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.collectDescendantsOfType
 49â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.nextLeaf
 50â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.prevLeaf
 51â†’
 52â†’/**
 53â†’ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 54â†’ *
 55â†’ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 56â†’ */
 57â†’public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 58â†’    StandardRule(
 59â†’        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 60â†’        visitorModifiers =
 61â†’            setOf(
 62â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 63â†’                    ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 64â†’                    mode = ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED,
 65â†’                ),
 66â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 67â†’            ),
 68â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY),
 69â†’    ) {
 70â†’    private var allowTrailingComma = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 71â†’
 72â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 73â†’        allowTrailingComma = editorConfig[TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY]
 74â†’    }
 75â†’
 76â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 77â†’        node: ASTNode,
 78â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80â†’    ) {
 81â†’        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 82â†’        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 83â†’        when (node.elementType) {
 84â†’            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 85â†’            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 86â†’            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 87â†’            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 88â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 89â†’            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90â†’            else -> Unit
 91â†’        }
 92â†’    }
 93â†’
 94â†’    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 95â†’        node: ASTNode,
 96â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 97â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 98â†’    ) {
 99â†’        val inspectNode =
100â†’            node
101â†’                .children()
102â†’                .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
103â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
104â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
105â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
106â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
107â†’            emit = emit,
108â†’        )
109â†’    }
110â†’
111â†’    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() = elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
112â†’
113â†’    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
114â†’        node: ASTNode,
115â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
116â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
117â†’    ) {
118â†’        val inspectNode =
119â†’            node
120â†’                .children()
121â†’                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122â†’                ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123â†’                return
124â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128â†’            emit = emit,
129â†’        )
130â†’    }
131â†’
132â†’    private fun visitValueList(
133â†’        node: ASTNode,
134â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
135â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136â†’    ) {
137â†’        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138â†’            node
139â†’                .children()
140â†’                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141â†’                ?.let { inspectNode ->
142â†’                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
143â†’                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
144â†’                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
145â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
146â†’                        emit = emit,
147â†’                    )
148â†’                }
149â†’        }
150â†’    }
151â†’
152â†’    private fun visitTypeList(
153â†’        node: ASTNode,
154â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156â†’    ) {
157â†’        val inspectNode =
158â†’            node
159â†’                .children()
160â†’                .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
161â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
162â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
163â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
164â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
165â†’            emit = emit,
166â†’        )
167â†’    }
168â†’
169â†’    private fun visitWhenEntry(
170â†’        node: ASTNode,
171â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
172â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
173â†’    ) {
174â†’        val psi = node.psi
175â†’        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
176â†’        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
177â†’            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
178â†’            return
179â†’        }
180â†’
181â†’        val inspectNode =
182â†’            node
183â†’                .children()
184â†’                .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
185â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
186â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
187â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
188â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
189â†’            emit = emit,
190â†’        )
191â†’    }
192â†’
193â†’    private fun visitClass(
194â†’        node: ASTNode,
195â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
196â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
197â†’    ) {
198â†’        val psi = node.psi
199â†’        require(psi is KtClass)
200â†’
201â†’        node
202â†’            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
203â†’            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
204â†’            ?.takeUnless { it.noEnumEntries() }
205â†’            ?.let { classBody ->
206â†’                classBody
207â†’                    .findNodeAfterLastEnumEntry()
208â†’                    ?.let { nodeAfterLastEnumEntry ->
209â†’                        when {
210â†’                            !node.isTrailingCommaAllowed() && nodeAfterLastEnumEntry.elementType == RBRACE -> {
211â†’                                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
212â†’                                    inspectNode = nodeAfterLastEnumEntry,
213â†’                                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = false,
214â†’                                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
215â†’                                    emit = emit,
216â†’                                )
217â†’                            }
218â†’
219â†’                            !classBody.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine() -> {
220â†’                                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
221â†’                                    inspectNode = nodeAfterLastEnumEntry,
222â†’                                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
223â†’                                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
224â†’                                    emit = emit,
225â†’                                )
226â†’                            }
227â†’                        }
228â†’                    }
229â†’            }
230â†’    }
231â†’
232â†’    private fun ASTNode.noEnumEntries() = children().none { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
233â†’
234â†’    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
235â†’        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
236â†’            children()
237â†’                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
238â†’                .toList()
239â†’                .takeLast(2)
240â†’
241â†’        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
242â†’    }
243â†’
244â†’    /**
245â†’     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
246â†’     *
247â†’     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
248â†’     * last element of the class.
249â†’     */
250â†’    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterLastEnumEntry() =
251â†’        children()
252â†’            .lastOrNull { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
253â†’            ?.children()
254â†’            ?.singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
255â†’            ?: lastChildNode
256â†’
257â†’    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
258â†’        inspectNode: ASTNode,
259â†’        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
260â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
261â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
262â†’    ) {
263â†’        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
264â†’        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
265â†’        val trailingCommaState =
266â†’            when {
267â†’                isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
268â†’                else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
269â†’            }
270â†’        when (trailingCommaState) {
271â†’            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
272â†’                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
273â†’                    inspectNode
274â†’                        .treeParent
275â†’                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
276â†’                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
277â†’                        ?.prevLeaf()
278â†’                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
279â†’                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
280â†’                                emit(
281â†’                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
282â†’                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
283â†’                                    true,
284â†’                                )
285â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
286â†’                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(inspectNode.treeParent.indent())
287â†’                                }
288â†’                            }
289â†’                        }
290â†’                } else {
291â†’                    emit(
292â†’                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
293â†’                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
294â†’                        true,
295â†’                    )
296â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
297â†’                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
298â†’                    }
299â†’                }
300â†’            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
301â†’                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
302â†’                    val leafBeforeArrowOrNull = leafBeforeArrowOrNull()
303â†’                    val addNewLine =
304â†’                        leafBeforeArrowOrNull
305â†’                            ?.let { !(leafBeforeArrowOrNull is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrowOrNull.textContains('\n')) }
306â†’                            ?: false
307â†’                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
308â†’                    if (addNewLine) {
309â†’                        emit(
310â†’                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
311â†’                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
312â†’                            true,
313â†’                        )
314â†’                    } else {
315â†’                        emit(
316â†’                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
317â†’                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
318â†’                            true,
319â†’                        )
320â†’                    }
321â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
322â†’                        if (addNewLine) {
323â†’                            val indent =
324â†’                                prevNode
325â†’                                    .treeParent
326â†’                                    .indent()
327â†’                            if (leafBeforeArrowOrNull is PsiWhiteSpace) {
328â†’                                (leafBeforeArrowOrNull as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(indent)
329â†’                            } else {
330â†’                                inspectNode
331â†’                                    .prevCodeLeaf()
332â†’                                    ?.nextLeaf()
333â†’                                    ?.let { before ->
334â†’                                        before.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(indent), before)
335â†’                                    }
336â†’                            }
337â†’                        }
338â†’
339â†’                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
340â†’                            val parentIndent =
341â†’                                (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
342â†’                                    ?: prevNode.indent()
343â†’                            (inspectNode as LeafPsiElement).apply {
344â†’                                this.treeParent.addChild(LeafPsiElement(COMMA, ","), this)
345â†’                                this.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(parentIndent), null)
346â†’                                this.treeParent.addChild(LeafPsiElement(SEMICOLON, ";"), null)
347â†’                            }
348â†’                            inspectNode.treeParent.removeChild(inspectNode)
349â†’                        } else {
350â†’                            inspectNode
351â†’                                .prevCodeLeaf()
352â†’                                ?.nextLeaf()
353â†’                                ?.let { before ->
354â†’                                    before.treeParent.addChild(LeafPsiElement(COMMA, ","), before)
355â†’                                }
356â†’                        }
357â†’                    }
358â†’                }
359â†’            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
360â†’                emit(
361â†’                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
362â†’                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
363â†’                    true,
364â†’                )
365â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
366â†’                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
367â†’                }
368â†’            }
369â†’            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
370â†’        }
371â†’    }
372â†’
373â†’    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean =
374â†’        when {
375â†’            element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
376â†’                isMultiline(element.parent)
377â†’            }
378â†’            element is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
379â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
380â†’            }
381â†’            element is KtWhenEntry -> {
382â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
383â†’            }
384â†’            element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> {
385â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
386â†’            }
387â†’            element is KtValueArgumentList &&
388â†’                element.children.size == 1 &&
389â†’                element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
390â†’                // special handling for collection literal
391â†’                // @Annotation([
392â†’                //    "something",
393â†’                // ])
394â†’                val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
395â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
396â†’            }
397â†’            element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> {
398â†’                false
399â†’            }
400â†’            else -> {
401â†’                element.textContains('\n')
402â†’            }
403â†’        }
404â†’
405â†’    private fun ASTNode.leafBeforeArrowOrNull() =
406â†’        when (psi) {
407â†’            is KtWhenEntry ->
408â†’                (psi as KtWhenEntry)
409â†’                    .arrow
410â†’                    ?.prevLeaf()
411â†’
412â†’            is KtFunctionLiteral ->
413â†’                (psi as KtFunctionLiteral)
414â†’                    .arrow
415â†’                    ?.prevLeaf()
416â†’
417â†’            else -> null
418â†’        }
419â†’
420â†’    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
421â†’        val codeLeaf =
422â†’            if (isCodeLeaf()) {
423â†’                this
424â†’            } else {
425â†’                prevCodeLeaf()
426â†’            }
427â†’        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
428â†’    }
429â†’
430â†’    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(
431â†’        from: PsiElement,
432â†’        to: PsiElement,
433â†’    ): Boolean {
434â†’        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
435â†’        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
436â†’            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
437â†’                return true
438â†’            }
439â†’            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
440â†’        }
441â†’        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
442â†’    }
443â†’
444â†’    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
445â†’        /**
446â†’         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
447â†’         */
448â†’        EXISTS,
449â†’
450â†’        /**
451â†’         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
452â†’         */
453â†’        MISSING,
454â†’
455â†’        /**
456â†’         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
457â†’         */
458â†’        NOT_EXISTS,
459â†’
460â†’        /**
461â†’         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
462â†’         */
463â†’        REDUNDANT,
464â†’    }
465â†’
466â†’    public companion object {
467â†’        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
468â†’
469â†’        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
470â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
471â†’                type =
472â†’                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
473â†’                        "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
474â†’                        "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
475â†’                            "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
476â†’                            "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
477â†’                            "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
478â†’                            "trailing comma when set.",
479â†’                        PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
480â†’                        BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
481â†’                    ),
482â†’                defaultValue = true,
483â†’                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = false,
484â†’            )
485â†’
486â†’        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE =
487â†’            TokenSet.create(
488â†’                CLASS,
489â†’                DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
490â†’                FUNCTION_LITERAL,
491â†’                FUNCTION_TYPE,
492â†’                TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
493â†’                VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
494â†’                WHEN_ENTRY,
495â†’            )
496â†’    }
497â†’}
498â†’
499â†’public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID: RuleId = TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule().ruleId
500â†’

{"new_string":"### Changed

* Change default code style to `ktlint_official`. Starting from this version, the default code style changes from `intellij_idea` (former `kotlin_official`) to `ktlint_official`. The `ktlint_official` codestyle adheres to the best parts of the Kotlin Coding Conventions and Android Kotlin styleguide. It no longer is restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter.

232â†’            )

239â†’    @Test
240â†’    fun `Given an editorconfig containing a property with undefined type then retrieving that property via the EditConfig may not result in an exception`() {
241â†’        // The Property and PropertyValue builders of the ec4j library do not allow to build a property having field "Property.type" set to
242â†’        // null. However, when loading an ".editorconfig" file containing a property with a name for which no property type exists with that
243â†’        // same name, this does result in such a property. It takes a detour to create the property:
244â†’        //  - Create an ".editorconfig" file on the file system mock
245â†’        //  - Load the ".editorconfig" via the EditorConfigDefaults loader
246â†’        //  - Find the property which is set in the ".editorconfig" file and validate that its type is null
247â†’        //  - Transform the property to a KtLint EditorConfig
248â†’        //  - Extract the property of the KtLint EditorConfig
249â†’        // Note that two properties are created which are identical except that only one of the properties has a null type and the other
250â†’        // property has a correctly defined type.
251â†’        //language=
252â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = "ktlint_test_rule-1"
253â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1)
254â†’        //language=
255â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = "ktlint_test_rule-2"
256â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2)
257â†’
258â†’        val ktlintTestFileSystem =
259â†’            KtlintTestFileSystem().apply {
260â†’                writeRootEditorConfigFile(
261â†’                    //language=EditorConfig
262â†’                    """
263â†’                    [*.{kt,kts}]
264â†’                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = disabled
265â†’                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = disabled
266â†’                    """.trimIndent(),
267â†’                )
268â†’            }
269â†’
270â†’        val ktlintTestRuleProperties =
271â†’            EditorConfigDefaults
272â†’                .load(
273â†’                    path = ktlintTestFileSystem.resolve(""),
274â†’                    propertyTypes =
275â†’                        setOf(
276â†’                            // Note that ktlintTestRuleEditorConfigPropertyType1 has been left out on purpose
277â†’                            ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2.type,
278â†’                        ),
279â†’                ).value
280â†’                .sections
281â†’                .flatMap { it.properties.values }
282â†’                .associateBy { it.name }
283â†’
284â†’        // Validate that both properties are loaded
285â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1] != null) {
286â†’            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
287â†’        }
288â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2] != null) {
289â†’            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
290â†’        }
291â†’
292â†’        // Validate that the type of one of the properties is null
293â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1]?.type == null) {
294â†’            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' should have an undefined type"
295â†’        }
296â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2]?.type != null) {
297â†’            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' should have a defined type"
298â†’        }
299â†’
300â†’        val editorConfig = EditorConfig(ktlintTestRuleProperties)
301â†’
302â†’        // Although the type of one of the properties is null, both can be loaded as the type of EditorConfigProperty is used to parse the
303â†’        // raw value of the property
304â†’        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
305â†’        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
306â†’    }
307â†’
308â†’    private fun editorConfigProperty(name: String) =
309â†’        EditorConfigProperty(
310â†’            name = name,
311â†’            type =
312â†’                PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
313â†’                    name,
314â†’                    "",
315â†’                    SafeEnumValueParser(RuleExecution::class.java),
316â†’                    RuleExecution.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
317â†’                ),
318â†’            defaultValue = RuleExecution.enabled,
319â†’        )
320â†’
321â†’    private companion object {
322â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_NAME = "some-property-name"
323â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE = "some-property-value"
324â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO = "some-property-value-android"
325â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT = "some-property-value-default"
326â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA = "some-property-value-intellij-idea"
327â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL = "some-property-value-official"
328â†’        val SOME_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY =
329â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
330â†’                name = SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
331â†’                type =
332â†’                    PropertyType(
333â†’                        SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
334â†’                        "",
335â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.IDENTITY_VALUE_PARSER,
336â†’                        setOf(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO, SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA),
337â†’                    ),
338â†’                defaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT,
339â†’                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO,
340â†’                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL,
341â†’                intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA,
342â†’            )
343â†’    }
344â†’}
345â†’

conversation_history.content:
1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5â†’import mu.KotlinLogging
  6â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9â†’
 10â†’private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14â†’ */
 15â†’public data class EditorConfig(
 16â†’    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17â†’) {
 18â†’    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19â†’        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20â†’    )
 21â†’
 22â†’    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23â†’        get() =
 24â†’            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25â†’                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26â†’                // the default value for the property
 27â†’                type
 28â†’                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29â†’                    .parsed
 30â†’                    ?: defaultValue
 31â†’            }
 32â†’
 33â†’    /**
 34â†’     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35â†’     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36â†’     */
 37â†’    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38â†’        when {
 39â†’            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40â†’                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41â†’                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42â†’                )
 43â†’
 44â†’            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45â†’                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46â†’        }
 47â†’        val property =
 48â†’            properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 49â†’                throw IllegalStateException(
 50â†’                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig " +
 51â†’                        "which is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the " +
 52â†’                        "property '${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 53â†’                )
 54â†’            }
 55â†’
 56â†’        editorConfigProperty
 57â†’            .propertyMapper
 58â†’            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 59â†’            ?.let { newValue ->
 60â†’                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 61â†’                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 62â†’                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 63â†’                    LOGGER.trace {
 64â†’                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 65â†’                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 66â†’                    }
 67â†’                }
 68â†’                return newValue
 69â†’            }
 70â†’
 71â†’        return if (property.isUnset) {
 72â†’            editorConfigProperty
 73â†’                .getDefaultValue()
 74â†’                .also {
 75â†’                    LOGGER.trace {
 76â†’                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 77â†’                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 78â†’                    }
 79â†’                }
 80â†’        } else {
 81â†’            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 82â†’            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 83â†’            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 84â†’            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 85â†’        }
 86â†’    }
 87â†’
 88â†’    /**
 89â†’     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 90â†’     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 91â†’     */
 92â†’    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 93â†’        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 94â†’        propertyName: String,
 95â†’    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 96â†’
 97â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 98â†’        when (codeStyle) {
 99â†’            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100â†’            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101â†’            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
102â†’        }
103â†’
104â†’    /**
105â†’     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
106â†’     */
107â†’    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
108â†’
109â†’    /**
110â†’     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
111â†’     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
112â†’     */
113â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
114â†’        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
115â†’
116â†’    /**
117â†’     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
118â†’     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
119â†’     */
120â†’    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
121â†’        propertyName: String,
122â†’        defaultValue: T,
123â†’    ): T =
124â†’        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
125â†’            .let { propertyValue ->
126â†’                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
127â†’                    propertyValue.parsed
128â†’                } else {
129â†’                    LOGGER.warn {
130â†’                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
131â†’                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
132â†’                    }
133â†’                    defaultValue
134â†’                }
135â†’            }
136â†’
137â†’    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
138â†’        parse(
139â†’            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
140â†’        )
141â†’
142â†’    /**
143â†’     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
144â†’     */
145â†’    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
146â†’        properties
147â†’            .values
148â†’            .map { mapper(it) }
149â†’
150â†’    /**
151â†’     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
152â†’     * not yet exist.
153â†’     */
154â†’    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
155â†’        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
156â†’        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
157â†’        return editorConfigProperties
158â†’            .defaultProperties()
159â†’            .plus(
160â†’                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
161â†’                properties,
162â†’            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
163â†’    }
164â†’
165â†’    /**
166â†’     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
167â†’     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
168â†’     * value of the active code style.
169â†’     */
170â†’    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
171â†’        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
172â†’        return editorConfigProperties
173â†’            .defaultProperties()
174â†’            .plus(
175â†’                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
176â†’                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
177â†’            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
178â†’    }
179â†’
180â†’    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
181â†’        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
182â†’            editorConfigProperties
183â†’                .groupBy { it.name }
184â†’                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
185â†’                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
186â†’                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
187â†’                        .count() > 1
188â†’                }
189â†’        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
190â†’            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
191â†’                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
192â†’                    editorConfigProperties
193â†’                        .joinToString(
194â†’                            separator = "\n",
195â†’                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
196â†’                        ) { "  - $it" }
197â†’                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
198â†’        }
199â†’    }
200â†’
201â†’    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
202â†’        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
203â†’            editorConfigProperty
204â†’                .writeDefaultValue()
205â†’                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
206â†’        }
207â†’    }
208â†’
209â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
210â†’
211â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
212â†’        Property
213â†’            .builder()
214â†’            .type(type)
215â†’            .name(name)
216â†’            .value(value)
217â†’            .build()
218â†’
219â†’    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
220â†’        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
221â†’}
222â†’
223â†’private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
224â†’    message: String,
225â†’) : RuntimeException(message)
226â†’

conversation_history.content:
0â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 1â†’
 2â†’!!! danger
 3â†’
 4â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 5â†’
 6â†’## Code style
 7â†’
 8â†’By default, the `ktlint_official` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `intellij_idea` or `android_studio`.
 9â†’
10â†’```ini
11â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
12â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
13â†’```
14â†’
15â†’## Disabled rules
16â†’
17â†’!!! note
18â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
19â†’
20â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
21â†’
22â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
23â†’```editorconfig
24â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
25â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
26â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
27â†’```
28â†’
29â†’Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
30â†’```editorconfig
31â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
32â†’```
33â†’
34â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
35â†’```editorconfig
36â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
37â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
38â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
39â†’```
40â†’
41â†’!!! note
42â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
43â†’
44â†’
45â†’## Final newline
46â†’
47â†’By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
48â†’
49â†’```ini
50â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
51â†’insert_final_newline = true
52â†’```
53â†’
54â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
55â†’
56â†’## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters
57â†’
58â†’Setting `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` forces a multiline function signature in case the function contains the specified minimum number of parameters even in case the function signature would fit on a single line. Use value `unset` (default) to disable this setting.
59â†’
60â†’!!! note
61â†’    By default, the `ktlint_official` code style wraps parameters of functions having at least 2 parameters. For other code styles, this setting is disabled by default. 
62â†’
63â†’```ini
64â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
65â†’ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than=unset
66â†’```
67â†’
68â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
69â†’
70â†’## Wrapping the expression body of a function
71â†’
72â†’Setting `ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping` determines if and when the expression body of a function is wrapped to a new line. This setting can be set to value `default`, `multiline` or `always`. 
73â†’
74â†’When set to `default`, the first line of a body expression is appended to the function signature as long as the max line length is not exceeded.
75â†’
76â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=default (or when not set)"
77â†’// Given that the function signature has to be written as a single line function signature
78â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
79â†’    .uppercase()
80â†’
81â†’// Given that the function signature has to be written as a multiline function signature
82â†’fun someFunction(
83â†’    a: Any,
84â†’    b: Any
85â†’): String = "some-result"
86â†’    .uppercase()
87â†’```
88â†’
89â†’When set to `multiline`, the body expression starts on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
90â†’
91â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=multiline"
92â†’// Given a single line body expression and
93â†’// a the function signature that has to be written as a single line function signature and
94â†’// it does not exceed the max line length
95â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result".uppercase()
96â†’
97â†’// Given a single line body expression and
98â†’// a the function signature that has to be written as a multiline function signature and
99â†’// it does not exceed the max line length

conversation_history.content:
1â†’## Annotation formatting
   2â†’
   3â†’Multiple annotations should be on a separate line than the annotated declaration; annotations with parameters should each be on separate lines; annotations should be followed by a space
   4â†’
   5â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
   6â†’
   7â†’    ```kotlin
   8â†’    // A single annotation (without parameters) is allowed on same line as annotated construct
   9â†’    @FunctionalInterface class FooBar {
  10â†’        @JvmField var foo: String
  11â†’        @Test fun bar() {}
  12â†’    }
  13â†’    // A class or function parameter may have a single annotation with parameter(s) on the same line
  14â†’    class Foo(@Path("fooId") val fooId: String)
  15â†’    class Bar(
  16â†’        @NotNull("fooId") val fooId: String,
  17â†’        @NotNull("bar") bar: String
  18â†’    )
  19â†’    // Multiple annotations (without parameters) are allowed on the same line
  20â†’    @Foo @Bar
  21â†’    class FooBar {
  22â†’        @Foo @Bar
  23â†’        var foo: String
  24â†’        @Foo @Bar
  25â†’        fun bar() {}
  26â†’    }
  27â†’    // An array of annotations (without parameters) is allowed on same line as annotated construct
  28â†’    @[Foo Bar] class FooBar2 {
  29â†’        @[Foo Bar] var foo: String
  30â†’        @[Foo Bar] fun bar() {}
  31â†’    }
  32â†’    ```
  33â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  34â†’
  35â†’    ```kotlin
  36â†’    // An annotation with parameter(s) is not allowed on same line as annotated construct
  37â†’    @Suppress("Unused") class FooBar {
  38â†’        @Suppress("Unused") var foo: String
  39â†’        @Suppress("Unused") fun bar() {}
  40â†’    }
  41â†’    // Multiple annotation on same line as annotated construct are not allowed
  42â†’    @Foo @Bar class FooBar {
  43â†’        @Foo @Bar var foo: String
  44â†’        @Foo @Bar fun bar() {}
  45â†’    }
  46â†’    ```
  47â†’
  48â†’Rule-id: `annotation` (`standard` rule set)
  49â†’
  50â†’## Argument list wrapping
  51â†’
  52â†’All arguments should be on the same line, or every argument should be on a separate line.
  53â†’
  54â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
  55â†’
  56â†’    ```kotlin
  57â†’    val x = f(
  58â†’        a,
  59â†’        b,
  60â†’        c
  61â†’    )
  62â†’    ```
  63â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  64â†’
  65â†’    ```kotlin
  66â†’    val x = f(
  67â†’        a,
  68â†’        b, c
  69â†’    )
  70â†’    ```
  71â†’
  72â†’Rule-id: `argument-list-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
  73â†’
  74â†’## Block comment initial star alignment
  75â†’
  76â†’Lines in a block comment which (exclusive the indentation) start with a `*` should have this `*` aligned with the `*` in the opening of the block comment.
  77â†’
  78â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
  79â†’
  80â†’    ```kotlin
  81â†’    /*
  82â†’     * This comment is formatted well.
  83â†’     */
  84â†’    ```
  85â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  86â†’
  87â†’    ```kotlin
  88â†’    /*
  89â†’          * This comment is not formatted well.
  90â†’        */
  91â†’    ```
  92â†’
  93â†’Rule id: `block-comment-initial-star-alignment` (`standard` rule set)
  94â†’
  95â†’## Chain wrapping
  96â†’
  97â†’When wrapping chained calls `.`, `?.` and `?:` should be placed on the next line
  98â†’
  99â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 100â†’
 101â†’    ```kotlin
 102â†’    val foo = listOf(1, 2, 3)
 103â†’        .filter { it > 2 }!!
 104â†’        .takeIf { it.count() > 100 }
 105â†’        ?.sum()
 106â†’    val foobar = foo()
 107â†’        ?: bar
 108â†’    ```
 109â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 110â†’
 111â†’    ```kotlin
 112â†’    val foo = listOf(1, 2, 3).
 113â†’        filter { it > 2 }!!.
 114â†’        takeIf { it.count() > 100 }?.
 115â†’        sum()
 116â†’    val foobar = foo() ?:
 117â†’        bar
 118â†’    ```
 119â†’
 120â†’Rule id: `chain-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 121â†’
 122â†’## Class/object naming
 123â†’
 124â†’Enforce naming of class.
 125â†’
 126â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 127â†’
 128â†’    ```kotlin
 129â†’    class Foo
 130â†’    class Foo1
 131â†’    ```
 132â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint JUnit Test"
 133â†’
 134â†’    ```kotlin
 135â†’    @Nested
 136â†’    inner class `Some descriptive class name` {
 137â†’        @Test
 138â†’        fun `Some descriptive test name`() {
 139â†’            // do something
 140â†’        }
 141â†’    }
 142â†’    ```
 143â†’
 144â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 145â†’
 146â†’    ```kotlin
 147â†’    class foo
 148â†’    class Foo_Bar
 149â†’    class `Some class in the production code`
 150â†’    ```
 151â†’
 152â†’!!! note
 153â†’    Functions in files which import a class from package `org.junit.jupiter.api` are considered to be test functions and are allowed to have a name specified between backticks and do not need to adhere to the normal naming convention. Although, the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) does not allow this explicitly for class identifiers, `ktlint` does allow it.
 154â†’
 155â†’This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `ClassName`.
 156â†’
 157â†’Rule id: `class-naming` (`standard` rule set)
 158â†’
 159â†’## Enum entry
 160â†’
 161â†’Enum entry names should be uppercase underscore-separated or upper camel-case separated.
 162â†’
 163â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 164â†’
 165â†’    ```kotlin
 166â†’    enum class Bar {
 167â†’        FOO,
 168â†’        Foo,
 169â†’        FOO_BAR,
 170â†’        FooBar
 171â†’    }
 172â†’    ```
 173â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 174â†’
 175â†’    ```kotlin
 176â†’    enum class Bar {
 177â†’        foo,
 178â†’        bAr,
 179â†’        Foo_Bar,
 180â†’    }
 181â†’    ```
 182â†’
 183â†’Rule id: `enum-entry-name-case` (`standard` rule set)
 184â†’
 185â†’## File name
 186â†’
 187â†’A file containing only one visible (e.g. non-private) class, and visible declarations related to that class only, should be named according to that element. The same applies if the file does not contain a visible class but exactly one type alias or one object declaration. Otherwise, the PascalCase notation should be used.
 188â†’
 189â†’Rule id: `filename` (`standard` rule set)
 190â†’
 191â†’## Final newline
 192â†’
 193â†’Ensures consistent usage of a newline at the end of each file. 
 194â†’
 195â†’This rule can be configured with `.editorconfig` property [`insert_final_newline`](../configuration-ktlint/#final-newline).
 196â†’
 197â†’Rule id: `final-newline` (`standard` rule set)
 198â†’
 199â†’## Import ordering
 200â†’
 201â†’Ensures that imports are ordered consistently (see [Import Layouts](../configuration-ktlint/#import-layouts) for configuration).
 202â†’
 203â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 204â†’
 205â†’    ```kotlin
 206â†’    import com.bar.Bar
 207â†’    import com.foo.Foo
 208â†’    import org.foo.bar.FooBar
 209â†’    import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap
 210â†’    ```
 211â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 212â†’
 213â†’    ```kotlin
 214â†’    import com.bar.Bar
 215â†’    import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap
 216â†’    import org.foo.bar.FooBar
 217â†’    import com.foo.Foo
 218â†’    ```
 219â†’
 220â†’Rule id: `import-ordering` (`standard` rule set)
 221â†’
 222â†’## Indentation
 223â†’
 224â†’Indentation formatting - respects `.editorconfig` `indent_size` with no continuation indent (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more).
 225â†’
 226â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 227â†’
 228â†’    ```kotlin
 229â†’    fun main() {
 230â†’        foobar(
 231â†’            a,
 232â†’            b,
 233â†’            c
 234â†’        )
 235â†’    }
 236â†’    ```
 237â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 238â†’
 239â†’    ```kotlin
 240â†’    fun main() {
 241â†’        foobar(
 242â†’              a,
 243â†’              b,
 244â†’              c
 245â†’              )
 246â†’    }
 247â†’    ```
 248â†’
 249â†’!!! note
 250â†’    This rule handles indentation for many different language constructs which can not be summarized with a few examples. See the [unit tests](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/blob/master/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleTest.kt) for more details.
 251â†’
 252â†’Rule id: `indent` (`standard` rule set)
 253â†’
 254â†’## Ktlint-suppression rule
 255â†’
 256â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported as of ktlint version `0.50.0`. This rule migrates the directives to Suppress or SuppressWarnings annotations.
 257â†’
 258â†’Identifiers in the @Suppress and @SuppressWarnings annotations to suppress ktlint rules are checked for validity and autocorrected when possible. 
 259â†’
 260â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 261â†’
 262â†’    ```kotlin
 263â†’    @file:Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-wildcard-imports")
 264â†’
 265â†’    class FooBar {
 266â†’        @Suppress("ktlint:standard:max-line-length")
 267â†’        val foo = "some longggggggggggggggggggg text"
 268â†’
 269â†’        fun bar() =
 270â†’            @Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-multi-spaces")
 271â†’            listOf(
 272â†’                "1   One", 
 273â†’                "10  Ten", 
 274â†’                "100 Hundred", 
 275â†’            )
 276â†’    }
 277â†’    ```
 278â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 279â†’
 280â†’    ```kotlin
 281â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard:no-wildcard-imports */
 282â†’
 283â†’    class FooBar {
 284â†’        val foo = "some longggggggggggggggggggg text" // ktlint-disable standard:max-line-length
 285â†’
 286â†’        fun bar() =
 287â†’            listOf(
 288â†’                /* ktlint-disable standard:no-multi-spaces */
 289â†’                "1   One", 
 290â†’                "10  Ten", 
 291â†’                "100 Hundred", 
 292â†’                /* ktlint-enable standard:no-multi-spaces */
 293â†’            )
 294â†’    }
 295â†’    ```
 296â†’
 297â†’Rule id: `ktlint-suppression` (`standard` rule set)
 298â†’
 299â†’!!! note
 300â†’    This rule can not be disabled in the `.editorconfig`.
 301â†’
 302â†’## Max line length
 303â†’
 304â†’Ensures that lines do not exceed the given length of `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more). This rule does not apply in a number of situations. For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to a comment that disables ktlint rules then that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.
 305â†’
 306â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 307â†’
 308â†’    ```kotlin
 309â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 310â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 311â†’    // Lines below are accepted although the max
 312â†’    // line length is exceeded.
 313â†’    package com.toooooooooooooooooooooooooooo.long
 314â†’    import com.tooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo.long
 315â†’    val foo =
 316â†’        """
 317â†’        fooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo
 318â†’        """
 319â†’    @Test
 320â†’    fun `Test description which is toooooooooooo long`() {
 321â†’    }
 322â†’    ```
 323â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 324â†’
 325â†’    ```kotlin
 326â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 327â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 328â†’    val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
 329â†’    val foooooooooooooo = "foooooooooooooooooooo" // some comment
 330â†’    val fooooooooooooo =
 331â†’        "foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo"
 332â†’    ```
 333â†’
 334â†’Rule id: `max-line-length` (`standard` rule set)
 335â†’
 336â†’## Modifier order

 346â†’        protected lateinit var lv: String

 359â†’Rule id: `modifier-order` (`standard` rule set)

 364â†’
 365â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 366â†’

 369â†’        if (true) {

 371â†’        } else {
 372â†’            return 1
 373â†’        }
 374â†’    ```
 375â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 376â†’
 377â†’    ```kotlin

 379â†’        if (true)
 380â†’            return 0
 381â†’        else
 382â†’            return 1
 383â†’    ```
 384â†’
 385â†’Rule id: `multiline-if-else` (`standard` rule set)

 515â†’## No line break after else
 516â†’
 517â†’Disallows line breaks after the else keyword if that could lead to confusion, for example:
 518â†’
 519â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 520â†’
 521â†’    ```kotlin
 522â†’    fun funA() {
 523â†’        if (conditionA()) {
 524â†’            doSomething()
 525â†’        } else if (conditionB()) {
 526â†’            doAnotherThing()
 527â†’        }
 528â†’    }
 529â†’    ```
 530â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 531â†’
 532â†’    ```kotlin
 533â†’    fun funA() {
 534â†’        if (conditionA()) {
 535â†’            doSomething()
 536â†’        } else
 537â†’        if (conditionB()) {
 538â†’            doAnotherThing()
 539â†’        }
 540â†’    }
 541â†’    ```
 542â†’
 543â†’Rule id: `no-line-break-after-else` (`standard` rule set)
 544â†’
 545â†’## No line break before assignment 
 546â†’
 547â†’When a line is broken at an assignment (`=`) operator the break comes after the symbol.
 548â†’
 549â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 550â†’
 551â†’    ```kotlin
 552â†’    val valA =
 553â†’        ""
 554â†’    ```
 555â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 556â†’
 557â†’    ```kotlin

 559â†’        = ""
 560â†’    ```
 561â†’
 562â†’Rule id: `no-line-break-before-assignment` (`standard` rule set)
 563â†’

 566â†’Except in indentation and in KDoc's it is not allowed to have multiple consecutive spaces.
 567â†’
 568â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 569â†’

 575â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 576â†’
 577â†’    ```kotlin
 578â†’    fun  main()  {
 579â†’        x(1,  3)
 580â†’    }
 581â†’    ```
 582â†’
 583â†’Rule id: `no-multi-spaces` (`standard` rule set)
 584â†’

 595â†’        bar()

 597â†’    ```

 605â†’
 606â†’        bar()

 615â†’

 618â†’## No `Unit` as return type 
 619â†’
 620â†’The `Unit` type is not allowed as return type of a function.
 621â†’returns (`fun fn {}` instead of `fun fn: Unit {}`)
 622â†’
 623â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 624â†’
 625â†’    ```kotlin
 626â†’    fun fn() {}
 627â†’    ```
 628â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 629â†’
 630â†’    ```kotlin

 632â†’    ```

 635â†’
 636â†’## No unused imports
 637â†’
 638â†’!!! warning
 639â†’    This rule is not able to detect *all* unused imports as mentioned in this [issue comment](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1754#issuecomment-1368201667).
 640â†’
 641â†’Rule id: `no-unused-imports` (`standard` rule set)
 642â†’
 643â†’## No wildcard imports
 644â†’
 645â†’No wildcard imports except imports listed in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`.
 646â†’
 647â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 648â†’
 649â†’    ```kotlin
 650â†’    import foobar.Bar
 651â†’    import foobar.Foo
 652â†’    ```
 653â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 654â†’
 655â†’    ```kotlin
 656â†’    import foobar.*
 657â†’    ```
 658â†’
 659â†’!!! warning
 660â†’    In case property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior. To disallow *all* wildcard imports, add property below to your `.editorconfig`:
 661â†’    ```editorconfig
 662â†’    [*.{kt,kts}]
 663â†’    ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = unset
 664â†’    ```
 665â†’
 666â†’Rule id: `no-wildcard-imports` (`standard` rule set)
 667â†’
 668â†’## Package name
 669â†’
 670â†’Validates that the package name matches the regular expression `[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*(\.[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*)*`.
 671â†’
 672â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 673â†’
 674â†’    ```kotlin
 675â†’    package foo
 676â†’    package foo.bar
 677â†’    ```
 678â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 679â†’
 680â†’    ```kotlin
 681â†’    package Foo
 682â†’    package foo.Foo
 683â†’    package `foo bar`
 684â†’    package foo.`foo bar`
 685â†’    ```
 686â†’
 687â†’Rule id: `package-name` (`standard` rule set)
 688â†’
 689â†’## Parameter list wrapping
 690â†’
 691â†’When class/function signature doesn't fit on a single line, each parameter must be on a separate line
 692â†’
 693â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 694â†’
 695â†’    ```kotlin
 696â†’    class ClassA(paramA: String, paramB: String, paramC: String)
 697â†’    class ClassA(
 698â†’        paramA: String,
 699â†’        paramB: String,
 700â†’        paramC: String
 701â†’    )
 702â†’    fun f(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any)
 703â†’    fun f(
 704â†’        a: Any,
 705â†’        b: Any,
 706â†’        c: Any
 707â†’    )
 708â†’    fun foo(
 709â†’        @Bar fooBar: FooBar
 710â†’    )
 711â†’    ```
 712â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed (ktlint_official)"
 713â†’
 714â†’    ```kotlin
 715â†’    class ClassA(
 716â†’        paramA: String, paramB: String,
 717â†’        paramC: String
 718â†’    )
 719â†’    fun f(
 720â†’        a: Any,
 721â†’        b: Any, c: Any
 722â†’    )
 723â†’    fun foo(@Bar fooBar: FooBar)
 724â†’    ```
 725â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed (non ktlint_official)""
 726â†’
 727â†’    ```kotlin
 728â†’    class ClassA(
 729â†’        paramA: String, paramB: String,
 730â†’        paramC: String
 731â†’    )
 732â†’    fun f(
 733â†’        a: Any,
 734â†’        b: Any, c: Any
 735â†’    )
 736â†’    ```

 738â†’Rule id: `parameter-list-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 739â†’

 748â†’    // at the X character on the right           X

 752â†’    )

 754â†’        fooooooooooooooooooooooooTooLong:
 755â†’            Foo,
 756â†’    )
 757â†’    ```

 759â†’
 760â†’    ```kotlin
 761â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 762â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 763â†’    class Bar(
 764â†’        val fooooooooooooooooooooooooTooLong:
 765â†’        Foo,
 766â†’    )

 769â†’        Foo,
 770â†’    )
 771â†’    ```
 772â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 773â†’
 774â†’    ```kotlin

 776â†’    // at the X character on the right           X

 786â†’
